llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm present: repositories adopting Cursor AI coding assistant. Control arm is matched never-treated repositories.
llm_extracted.notes: This is a difference-in-differences natural experiment using propensity score matching. The regression coefficient 0.0260 represents the treatment effect. The outcome is count data (commits), and given the small coefficient magnitude (~2.6%) and the fact that the effect_in_outcome_units matches the coefficient closely, this appears to be a log-transformed or asinh-transformed outcome (common for count data in DiD models). Treating as log-transformed. The verbatim quote shows ±4.40% which appears to be a confidence interval width but is not explicitly labeled as 95% CI bounds, so not populating CI fields. Additional time-varying effects reported for months 1 and 2 show larger initial impacts (+55.4% and +14.5%) but the primary overall effect is +2.63%.
source_quotes: The models estimate a 55.4% increase in commits in the first month, a 14.5% increase in commits in the second month
Repositories adopting Cursor, identified by committing Cursor configuration files to their git system between January 2024 and March 2025.
Matched "never-treated" repositories not using Cursor during the observation period, selected via propensity score matching.
